forecast-period.text: A high pressure system south of the Bight extends a ridge over SA. The high will slowly move east over the weekend ahead of a cold front which will slip to the south of SA and weaken. The high will remain near stationary in the southern Tasman Sea, maintaining a ridge across the state early next week.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.
Northeasterly 15 to 20 knots turning northerly 10 to 15 knots in the evening.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2.5 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres by early evening.
Below 0.5 metres, tending southerly 1 to 1.5 metres during the afternoon.
Mostly sunny. The chance of a thunderstorm west of Kangaroo Island in the late afternoon and evening.
Northwest to northeasterly 10 to 15 knots tending northwest to southwesterly in the early morning then tending southeast to southwesterly in the morning.
Southerly 1.5 metres, increasing to 1.5 to 2 metres west of Kangaroo Island.
Partly cloudy. The chance of a thunderstorm in the morning.
South to southeasterly 10 to 15 knots turning easterly during the day.
